codeReview.summary: Clean, well-scoped implementation. Replaces F1 context switch with Ctrl+Tab in the compositor by adding KEY_TAB/KEY_LEFTCTRL constants, a ctrl_pressed state variable, and modifier-gated context switch logic. The old KEY_F1 constant and F1 check are fully removed. Ctrl key events are consumed by the compositor (not forwarded to editor), Tab alone falls through to normal editor input, and only Tab+Ctrl triggers the switch. Two new tests replace one old test, covering keycode mapping correctness and the boolean Ctrl+Tab logic. All five expected behaviors are met: Ctrl+Tab switches, F1 no longer switches, modifier state is tracked, Tab alone doesn't switch, and existing tests pass. Visual verification in QEMU was thorough (5 interactive checks covering all key combinations).

issues.description: architecture.md still references 'toggled via F1' (line 17) and 'F1 key switches modes' (line 76). The AGENTS.md coding conventions state: 'When changing a constant value, search for ALL comments and docstrings referencing the old value and update them.' These shared-state docs weren't updated to reflect the Ctrl+Tab change. This is stale documentation, not a code bug.

Left Ctrl events are unconditionally consumed (continue), meaning the editor never sees Ctrl key state. Currently harmless since the editor has no Ctrl+key bindings, but if future features add Ctrl+C/Z/V, the compositor will need to forward Ctrl events for non-Tab combos. Not a bug in current scope — just a design consideration for future modifier-aware editing features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md references F1 as the context switch key in two places (line 17: 'toggled via F1', line 76: 'F1 key switches modes') but the feature changed this to Ctrl+Tab. AGENTS.md convention says to update all references when changing constants, but this rule only covers code comments/docstrings — it doesn't explicitly mention .factory/library/ docs. The worker correctly updated the serial log message and code comments but missed the library docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lines 17 and 76 still reference F1. Diff shows code comments and serial message were updated. AGENTS.md says: 'When changing a constant value, search for ALL comments and docstrings referencing the old value and update them.'

summary: Pass. The Ctrl+Tab context switch implementation is correct, well-tested, and visually verified. The compositor cleanly tracks Left Ctrl modifier state and gates context switching on Ctrl+Tab rather than F1. Two unit tests cover the keycode mappings and boolean logic. One non-blocking documentation issue: .factory/library/architecture.md still references F1 in two places. No code bugs found.
